ocr.text: Understanding the distribution of blood pressure levels across various populations is crucial for assessing cardiovascular health
and informing public health strategies. The data presented in the table offers a comparative analysis of systolic and diastolic
blood pressure measurements among different geographic categories, including Bear River, Green River, and Polecat Creek.
Notably, the metrics for systolic blood pressure (mm Hg) and diastolic blood pressure (mm Hg) reveal significant variations

across these regions, which may be influenced by environmental, lifestyle, and genetic factors unique to each population. 

For
instance, the Bear River category exhibits a notably low incidence of individuals with systolic readings below the normal
threshold of 120 mm Hg, highlighting potential health risks associated with elevated blood pressure levels in this area.
Conversely, the Green River and Polecat Creek populations show differing patterns that warrant further investigation.
Understanding these disparities not only aids in developing targeted interventions for hypertension management but also
emphasizes the importance of localized health assessments. This data underscores the necessity for continuous monitoring and
tailored public health initiatives to address the specific needs of each community.
Category  Systolic blood pressure (mm Hg)  Diastolic blood pressure (mm Hg)
Normal blood presssurea  <120  And  <80
Prehypertension  Stage 1  120–129  Or  80–84
Hypertension  Stage 1  140–159  Or  90–99
Stage 2  ≥160  Or  ≥100
Isolated systolic hypertension  ≥140  And  <90
Population  Bear River  Bear River F1  Green River  Green River F1  Polecat Creek  Polecat Creek F1
